Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 024 [Incantations for having well-wishers][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢EN₂⸣ am-min₄-i₃ ZIta₅ GIM UR.MAḪ

(o 1) Incantation. "Why are you attacking like a lion, infuriated like a wolf, gathered the assembly like a cultic performer?

o 22

ḫu-uz-zu-za-a-ta₅ GIM UR.BAR.RA

o 33

UKKIN pu-uḫ-ḫu-ra-a-ta₅ GIM lu₂KUR.GAR.RA

o 44

ana-ku ša₂ i-du-u₂ i-te-pu-uš ina A-MEŠ am-te-si

(o 4) I have done what I know: I have washed my hands in water, I have been anointed with good oil.

o 55

ŠU-MINa-a at-ta-ap-ša₂-aš₂ I₃.GIŠ DU₁₀.GA ana-ku

o 66

ina la-mi-ki a-na DUMU.MUNUS šak-na ša₂ BAD₃.ANki

(o 6) Surrounded by you, I have become hostile to the daughter of the governor of Der (and) to the daughter of the mayor of Emutbal. I have flattened (myself) before you.

o 77

a-na DUMU.MUNUS lu₂ḫa-za-nu ša₂ EME.DU.BA.LU

o 88

ag-ta-ra-aʾ ak-ta-ba-as-ku-nu-ši

o 99

GIM A-MEŠ uṭ-ṭa-ab-bi-ku-nu-ši GIM gišMA₂

(o 9) As (in) water I have sunk before you. Like a ship when it lands I have sat down alongside you.

o 1010

ki-i e-lu-u₂ at-ta-šab ana ⸢muḫ-ḫi⸣-ku-nu

o 1111

KA-ku-nu ana BADe NUNDUN-ku-nu ana ka-ta-mu

(o 11) I will not permit your mouth to open, your lips to close, your tongue to speak!" Formula of the incantation.

o 1212

EME-ku-nu ana da-ba-bu ul a-nam-din te EN₂

o 1313

KA.INIM.MA IGI-BI ḪUL₂.LA.KE₄ DU₃.DU₃-BI EN₂ an-nit

(o 13) Incantation for he who sees him to rejoice.

(o 13) Its ritual: you recite this incantation 3 times towards the centre of the good oil and you anoint your face, your arms and your chest, and those who see you will be joyful on seeing you.

o 1414

3-šu₂ ana ŠA₃ I₃ DU₁₀.GA ŠID-ma IGI-ki a-ḫi-ki

o 1515

u bi-rit a-ḫi-ki ŠEŠ₂-ma a-mi-ru-ka ana IGI-ka  ; ḫa-du-u

o 1616

ana a-mir-šu₂ ana IGI-šu₂ ḫa-de-e KU.KU gišMES.MA₂.KAN.NA

(o 16) For one who sees him to be joyful on seeing him: seeds from the Magan-tree, ivory from the elephant, red gold, (and) lapis lazuli (wrapped) in leather.

o 1717

ZU₂ AM.SI KU₃.SI₂₂ ḪUŠ na₄ZA.GIN₃ ina KUŠ

o 1818

ina itiAPIN U₄ 20-KAM U₄.DA-KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL

(o 18) In Arahsamnu (Month VIII) the 20th day is the fixed date (for the rituals) to change (someone's) mood (and the rituals for) the prince in his palace to remember his name with kindness.

o 1919

NUN ina E₂.GAL-šu₂ MU-šu₂ ana SIG₅ti₃ ḫa-sa-sa

o 2020

U₄ 21-KAM MIN<(U₄.DA-KAM)> a-mir-ka ana IGI-ka ḫa-de-e u ra-a-ši

(o 20) The 21st day is ditto (the fixed date) (for the rituals for) one who sees you to be joyful and rejoice on seeing you.

Reverse
r 1r 1

⸢EN₂⸣ ma-la 1 DANNA IM.LAGAB i₃-dig-lat a-na 2 DANNA

(r 1) Incantation. For as much as 10 miles of mud on the Tigris to 20 miles of mud on the Euphrates there is a din.

r 22

IM.LAGAB pu-rat-tu₂ ik-kil-lu ruq-qe₂-e-tu₂

(r 2) Female turtles from the river (and) female tortoises from the dry land beat their weapons towards the centre of the river.

r 33

ša₂ ID₂ še-lep-pu-ta-tu₄ ša₂ ta-ba-lu

r 44

ana ŠA₃ ID₂ kak-ki-ši-na i-rap-pi-sa

r 55

ak-ka-aʾ-i la tap-laḫ te-ti-iqtiq₂

(r 5) How had you no fear as you passed among them?

r 66

ina bi-ri-ši-na ana-ku na₄as-ḫar ŠEŠ₂-ka

(r 6) I am anointed with ashar-stone.

r 77

na₄as-ḫar ra-am-ka-<ka> na₄as-ḫar GIM na-al-šu₂

(r 7) I am bathed with ashar-stone.

(r 7) My neck is encircled with ashar-stone like dew and mist.

r 88

u₃ MURU₉ la-ma-a ti-ik-ki

r 99

šu-us-ḫur IRI šu-us-ḫur E₂ šu-us-ḫur

(r 9) The city is put to flight, the estate is put to flight, my adversary is put to flight, so that they follow me.

r 1010

EN DU₁₁.DU₁₁-ia-a-ma il-la-ku ina EGIR-ia₅

r 1111

KA.INIM.MA IGI-BI ḪUL₂.LA.KE₄ DU₃.DU₃-BI

(r 11) Incantation for those that see him to rejoice.

(r 11) Its ritual: you pound ashar-stone in oil. You recite the incantation on top. You anoint your face, your arms and your chest, and wherever he goes he will be favoured.

r 1212

na₄as-ḫar ina I₃.GIŠ SUD₂ EN₂ 3-šu₂ ana UGU

r 1313

ŠIDnu IGI-ka A₂-MEŠ-ka bir-ta a-ḫi-ka

r 1414

ŠEŠ₂-ma e-ma il-lak ma-gir

r 1515

ana KI DUku ma-ga-ri-šu₂ šimGUG₂.GUG₂ u₂IGI-lim  ; ina KUŠ

(r 15) For his being favoured where he goes: kukuru-aromatic (and) imhur-līm-plant (wrapped) in leather.

r 1616

EN₂ šak-na-ka kušE.SIR₂ ina GIR₃-MIN-ia₅ ina IGI-ka aš₂-ba-ak

(r 16) Incantation. I have put sandals on my feet (and) I am seated in front of you.

r 1717

a-ṣa-ḫu ṣu-u₂-ḫu a-a-ri IGI-ia₂ ḪI.LI IGI-MIN-ia₂

(r 17) The laugh I laugh is the rosette of my face, the attractiveness of my eyes.

r 1818

KI.AG₂!.GA₂ E₂.GAR₈ ana-ku-si-in-na-tu-um-ma

(r 18) ... the wall, I am celebrations, alternatively a spear, and everything that I say to you is amusing.

r 1919

⸢ša₂⸣-niš si-in-na-tu-um-ma mim-ma ma-la  ; a-qab-ak-ka ṣe?-e-ḫi

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

DU₃.DU₃-BI EN₂ an-nit ana muḫ-ḫi kušE.SIR₂

(t.e. 1) Its ritual: You recite this incantation over the sandal. You put (it) on your feet and where you go people will be joyful towards you.

t.e. 22

ŠIDnu ina GIR₃-MIN-ka GARan-ma KI DUku  ; ḪUL₂-MEŠ-⸢ka⸣
